Documentation on using Flask-Caching to improve application performance with caching techniques.

# Purpose
Caching is a technique used to improve the performance of an application by storing the results of time-consuming operations for a specified duration. This allows the application to reuse the stored results instead of recalculating them, which can be beneficial if the results remain valid for a short period, such as 5 minutes. Flask, a web framework, does not include built-in caching capabilities. However, the `Flask-Caching` extension provides this functionality and supports various caching backends. Developers can also create custom caching backends to suit specific needs. For more information on `Flask-Caching`, refer to the official documentation at the provided link.
